environment_option_rationale: Option A (Supabase branch) and option B (temporary dedicated Supabase project) both require the Supabase CLI and/or a Supabase Management API personal access token; neither is installed or present in .env.local/.env.mission (only SUPABASE_URL + SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY for the existing Production project, i.e. PostgREST-level access, no ability to create a new project or branch). Option C was used instead: a genuine local PostgreSQL 18.2 server (not PGlite/WASM this time -- a real Windows-native Postgres binary already present at 'C:\Program Files\PostgreSQL\18', the same engine and version previously used and accepted in AKARFINDER-OPENSERP-TO-SUPABASE-FIRST-WRITE-1's rollback rehearsal, see docs/OPENSERP_FIRST_WRITE_ROLLBACK_READINESS_1.md), on a fresh disposable data directory and a non-default port, with a role model explicitly reproducing Supabase's anon / authenticated / service_role roles and their RLS bypass semantics.
engine: PostgreSQL 18.2 (native Windows binary, local, disposable data directory, ephemeral, dropped after the gate)
postgres_version_query_result: PostgreSQL 18.2 on x86_64-windows, compiled by msvc-19.44.35222, 64-bit

instance.auth: trust (local-only disposable instance, no password used, no network exposure beyond localhost)
instance.data_directory: scratch project (outside all git worktrees), deleted after the gate

role_model.anon: NOLOGIN, NOBYPASSRLS -- matches Supabase's anon role (subject to RLS)
role_model.authenticated: NOLOGIN, NOBYPASSRLS -- matches Supabase's authenticated role (subject to RLS)
role_model.service_role: NOLOGIN, BYPASSRLS -- matches Supabase's service_role role (bypasses RLS entirely, exactly as this project's own docs/MARKET_INDEX_SECURITY_AND_RLS.md documents for the existing tables)
role_model.grants: USAGE on schema public + SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on all tables granted to all three roles, matching Supabase's actual default grant pattern -- the real access boundary is enforced by RLS, not by table-level grants.

migration_apply_status: PASS -- all 5 applied cleanly against real PostgreSQL 18.2, 0 errors (2 expected NOTICEs from 'drop constraint if exists' on constraints that did not pre-exist, harmless)

test_1_anon_select_new_tables.expected: 0 rows from all 4 new tables, no error

test_2_authenticated_select_new_tables.expected: 0 rows from all 4 new tables, no error

test_3_anon_insert_rejected.expected: INSERT rejected by RLS (no permissive policy exists)
test_3_anon_insert_rejected.actual: ERROR: new row violates row-level security policy for table "discovery_candidates"

test_4_authenticated_insert_rejected.actual: ERROR: new row violates row-level security policy for table "source_offer_observations"

test_5_service_role_bypasses_rls.actual: INSERT 0 1 succeeded; subsequent SELECT as service_role saw 1 row in discovery_candidates

test_6_listing_sources_access_unchanged.expected: migration 5 (additive columns only) does not alter listing_sources RLS/access model
test_6_listing_sources_access_unchanged.actual: anon count=0, service_role count=0 -- consistent with the stand-in's baseline RLS state; no new policy was introduced by the migration

test_7_rls_enabled_zero_policies.expected: rowsecurity=true and 0 rows in pg_policies for all 4 new tables
test_7_rls_enabled_zero_policies.actual: all 4 tables: rowsecurity=true; pg_policies query returned 0 rows for all of them

append_only_db_level_followup_from_gate_.detail: This gate confirms RLS blocks anon/authenticated INSERT entirely (test 3/4) and that only service_role (BYPASSRLS) can write, matching Production's real access model where server code exclusively uses the service-role key. Whether service_role itself is additionally prevented from UPDATE/DELETE on source_offer_observations at the raw SQL level was intentionally left unenforced by design (per docs/MARKET_INDEX_SECURITY_AND_RLS.md: 'append-only is enforced by the application's own type surface' via the repository interface, not by a DB-level REVOKE) -- this is a documented, accepted design choice from the original foundation mission, not a new gap found here.
